title: Advanced Selectors
description: Fix a stylesheet that uses overly specific selectors by refactoring to :is(), :where(), and :not() pseudo-classes
html:
Main Heading

Sub Heading

Section Heading

Introduction paragraph with special styling.

Regular paragraph text.

  • Feature one
  • Feature two (disabled)
  • Feature three

validationRules.message: Intro paragraph should be dark gray, not the lighter gray
Disabled items should not be bold

hints.text: :is() groups selectors with the highest specificity of the list. :where() does the same but with zero specificity. :not() excludes matches.
Use ':is(h1, h2, h3)' to group headings. Use 'p:not(.intro)' for non-intro paragraphs. Use 'li:not(.disabled)' for active list items.
Group with :is(), exclude with :not(), and use :where() for zero-specificity defaults.
